quiz.question:
White's center is quite strong. 

Keeping pawn breaks in mind, what should you play?

feedback.text: If you have time to challenge a central pawn, you should. This move poses White many problems.

answers.comment: undermining the center, achieving a pawn break.

feedback.text: ...c5 is too far away. Let's do something else.

distractors.comment: adding another piece to defend c7, maybe preparing a future c5.

feedback.text: This move is too premature. We won't even have time to win the pawn so we have to do something else.

distractors.comment: getting rid of the strong knight.

feedback.text: You have to focus on challenging the central pawns first.

distractors.comment: putting more pressure on the knight.
